title: HRM-Text: Efficient Pretraining Beyond Scaling
abstract: HRM-Text is a 1B-parameter hierarchical recurrent language model trained from scratch on instruction-response pairs using a task-completion objective and PrefixLM masking. It combines a dual-timescale HRM architecture with MagicNorm and warmup deep credit assignment to stabilize recurrent depth for language modeling. Trained on 40B unique tokens with a reported budget around $1,500, HRM-Text achieves strong reasoning-heavy benchmark results such as 60.7% MMLU, 81.9% ARC-C, 82.2% DROP, 84.5% GSM8K, and 56.2% MATH, suggesting that architecture and objective co-design can improve compute-to-performance efficiency.
publication_status: No formal conference or journal reference found as of 2026-06-06
